plant_profile.history: Recognized in traditional medicine systems globally. Lemon Balm has been documented in classical texts for its therapeutic applications. It has a rich history of use in both folk remedies and standardized Ayurvedic formulations.
plant_profile.physical_characteristics: Lemon Balm exhibits distinctive morphological features typical of medicinal herbs used in traditional pharmacopoeias. It is harvested carefully to preserve its active constituents.
plant_profile.distribution: Cultivated and wildcrafted in suitable climatic regions globally, with specific sources standardized by pharmacopoeias.

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_modulation: Primarily pacifies Kapha and Vata doshas due to its heating and drying qualities. Excessive use may aggravate Pitta.

medicinal_uses.digestive_health: Promotes healthy digestion, alleviates bloating, and supports gastrointestinal motility. Lemon Balm helps balance digestive fire (Agni).
medicinal_uses.respiratory_support: Assists in clearing the respiratory tract and relieving occasional congestion. Widely used for upper respiratory comfort.
medicinal_uses.systemic_inflammation: Contains active phytochemicals that help modulate inflammatory responses in the body, supporting joint and tissue health.
medicinal_uses.stress_and_vitality: Acts as an adaptogenic or restorative tonic in some formulations, helping the body cope with environmental stressors.

standardization.api_reference: Standardized according to WHO guidelines and the Ayurvedic Pharmacopoeia of India for Lemon Balm.

preparation_methods.decoction: Boil 1 part of the coarse powder with 16 parts of water, reduced to 4 parts. Filter and consume fresh.
preparation_methods.powder: Fine powder (filtered through 80 mesh) taken with warm water, honey, or ghee depending on the condition.
preparation_methods.infusion: Steep the raw herb in hot water for 10-15 minutes to extract delicate volatile oils.
preparation_methods.topical: Made into a paste (Lepa) with water or oil and applied externally for localized relief.

dosage.adults: Powder: 3-6g per day in divided doses. Decoction: 15-30ml twice daily.
dosage.children: 1/4 or 1/2 of the adult dose under the strict guidance of an Ayurvedic physician.
dosage.elderly: Start with lower doses and monitor. Adjust according to digestive capacity (Agni).

contraindications: Always consult a physician before use during pregnancy or lactation.
Avoid in cases of severe Pitta aggravation or active bleeding disorders.
side_effects: May cause mild gastric upset if taken on an empty stomach.
High doses may lead to unwanted purgation or heating sensations.
drug_interactions: May interact with anticoagulant or antihypertensive medications. Professional supervision is advised when combining with western pharmaceuticals.
safety.toxicity_notes: GRAS Status: Generally recognized as safe when consumed in recommended therapeutic dosages based on WHO monographs.
